The deal structure and stake percentage have not been disclosed; the report is based on unnamed sources and no definitive agreement has been announced. If completed, the transaction would give Samsung significant exposure to Mistral's open-weight LLM technology and European AI ecosystem.

deals.note: All-stock merger of game engine and monetization at trough currencies; AppLovin’s rival $17.5B all-stock bid was rejected the following month.

deals.name: Hellman & Friedman & Permira / Zendesk
deals.note: $77.50/share — months after Zendesk's board rejected $127-132/share. The sharpest lesson of 2022: the bid you refuse in February may not exist in June.

deals.name: Polestar / Gores Guggenheim
deals.note: ~$20B EV de-SPAC completed after the window had shut — priced in the old world, listed into the new one.

deals.name: Broadcom / VMware
deals.note: Largest software takeover in history: announced May 2022, closed Nov 2023 after an 18-month global regulatory gauntlet. Broadcom immediately restructured VMware around subscription licensing.

deals.name: Kaseya / Datto
deals.note: Insight-backed strategic take-private of an MSP software rival — consolidation logic, PE money.

deals.name: Elon Musk / Twitter
deals.note: $54.20/share. Musk tried to walk, Delaware Chancery forced the close in Oct 2022. $13B of the debt stayed hung on bank books for two years — a league-table win and a balance-sheet loss at once.

deals.name: Twitter buyout financing
deals.note: $13B committed debt for the Musk buyout that markets then refused to take — it sat on bank balance sheets for over two years, the era’s defining hung-debt story.

deals.name: Thoma Bravo / SailPoint
deals.note: $65.25/share cash; identity security consolidation begins — SailPoint later re-IPO'd in 2025, a full PE round trip.

deals.name: Thoma Bravo / Anaplan
deals.note: Signed at $66/share, then repriced DOWN to $63.75 after a closing-condition dispute — a rare mid-deal price cut that previewed the 2022 reset.

deals.name: Google / Mandiant
deals.note: $23/share cash into Google Cloud — threat intelligence as a cloud differentiator, the Wiz thesis eight years early... or three.

deals.name: Intel / Tower Semiconductor
deals.note: Foundry acquisition abandoned Aug 2023 when Chinese approval never came; Intel paid a $353M breakup fee — geopolitics as deal risk, pre-export-control era.

deals.name: Vista & Elliott / Citrix
deals.note: $104/share LBO signed at the top; the hung debt from this financing sat on bank balance sheets for a year — the deal that froze the LBO market it led.

deals.name: Microsoft / Activision Blizzard
deals.note: The defining regulatory saga of the era: sued by the FTC, blocked then cleared by the UK CMA after a cloud-gaming restructure, closed Oct 2023 — 21 months after signing. Every merger agreement since has priced that timeline risk.

deals.name: Take-Two / Zynga
deals.note: Cash-and-stock mobile-gaming consolidation announced a week before Microsoft/Activision — the gaming M&A wave in miniature.

deals.name: Oracle / Cerner
deals.note: $95/share all cash; closed Jun 2022. Oracle betting the cloud transition on healthcare records.

deals.name: Samsara
deals.note: NYSE: IOT. The last major listing before the window slammed shut for 22 months.

deals.name: HashiCorp
deals.note: Nasdaq: HCP. $14B at pricing; acquired by IBM in 2025 for $6.4B — this tracker holds both ends of the round trip.

deals.name: Nubank
deals.note: NYSE: NU. Buffett-backed Brazilian neobank at $41B — briefly worth more than Itaú; the emerging-markets fintech peak.

deals.name: Permira / Mimecast
deals.note: Early entry in what became the 2022 security take-private wave.

deals.name: Grab / Altimeter Growth
deals.note: Largest de-SPAC ever at ~$40B — Southeast Asia’s superapp chose the SPAC route over an IPO at the very top.

deals.name: Lucid — convertible notes
deals.note: ~$1.75B converts due 2026 at peak EV enthusiasm. (Figures approximate.)

deals.name: Ericsson / Vonage
deals.note: Telecom-equipment giant buying cloud communications APIs; closed Jul 2022 into a very different market than it was struck in.

deals.name: Hellman & Friedman & Bain / Athenahealth
deals.note: Sponsor-to-sponsor flip at nearly 3x the 2019 take-private price — the secondary-buyout machine at full speed. Closed Feb 2022.

deals.name: Peloton — equity offering
deals.note: Emergency-flavored raise days after guiding down — the first big 'the boom is over' follow-on.

deals.name: Rivian
deals.note: Nasdaq: RIVN. Largest US IPO since Alibaba — $66.5B at pricing, briefly >$150B, on ~zero revenue. The last full expression of ZIRP-era pricing; nothing raised more until it.

deals.name: Affirm — convertible notes
deals.note: 0% coupon converts due 2026 — free money at the peak. The same paper became a case study in convert-holder pain within a year.

deals.name: Advent-led group / McAfee
deals.note: $26/share consortium take-private (Advent, Permira, CPP, GIC, Abu Dhabi) — peak-cycle club deal, closed Mar 2022.

deals.name: GlobalFoundries
deals.note: Nasdaq: GFS. Mubadala floated the foundry at ~$26B — semiconductor sovereignty listing two years before the CHIPS Act money flowed.

deals.name: WeWork / BowX
deals.note: Public at ~$9B two years after the failed $47B IPO; Chapter 11 followed in Nov 2023 — the full arc of the era in one ticker.

deals.name: GitLab
deals.note: Nasdaq: GTLB. All-remote DevOps platform; priced above range, popped 35%.

deals.name: Toast
deals.note: NYSE: TOST. Priced above a raised range at ~$20B for a restaurant-software company — the vintage's valuation exuberance distilled.

deals.name: Freshworks
deals.note: Nasdaq: FRSH. First Indian SaaS company to list on a US exchange.

deals.name: Intuit / Mailchimp
deals.note: Largest-ever purchase of a bootstrapped software company — no VC on the cap table, $12B in cash and stock.

deals.name: Square / Afterpay
deals.note: All-stock at the BNPL peak; worth ~$13.8B by the Jan 2022 close as both stocks fell — exchange-ratio risk in one chart.

deals.name: Robinhood
deals.note: Nasdaq: HOOD. Priced at the bottom of the range, fell 8% day one, then meme-rallied 80% the next week — peak 2021 in a single chart.

deals.name: Duolingo
deals.note: Nasdaq: DUOL. Priced above range — consumer-subscription quality that outlasted the vintage.

deals.name: Lucid / Churchill Capital IV
deals.note: The most hyped SPAC of the mania closed days into this window at a ~$24B valuation — CCIV retail frenzy as the era’s emblem.
